# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

# app/mailers/sendgrid_mailer.rb
class SendgridMailer < ActionMailer::Base
  default from: -> { default_from_address }

  def self.default_from_address
    ENV.fetch("DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL", "no-reply@example.com")
  end

  def notify(to:, subject:, html_body: nil, text_body: nil, from: nil, categories: [], custom_args: {})
    raise ArgumentError, "Recipient email is required" if to.blank?
    raise ArgumentError, "Subject is required" if subject.blank?
    raise ArgumentError, "Either html_body or text_body must be provided" if html_body.blank? && text_body.blank?

    headers["X-SMTPAPI"] = build_smtpapi_header(categories: categories, custom_args: custom_args).to_json

    mail(
      to: to,
      from: from.presence || self.class.default_from_address,
      subject: subject
    ) do |format|
      format.text { render plain: text_body } if text_body.present?
      format.html { render html: html_body.html_safe } if html_body.present?
    end
  end

  private

  def build_smtpapi_header(categories:, custom_args:)
    payload = {}
    payload[:category] = Array(categories) if categories.present?
    payload[:unique_args] = custom_args.stringify_keys if custom_args.present?
    payload
  end
end

# config/initializers/sendgrid.rb
sendgrid_api_key = ENV["SENDGRID_API_KEY"]

ActionMailer::Base.smtp_settings = {
  address: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_SMTP_ADDRESS", "smtp.sendgrid.net"),
  port: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_SMTP_PORT", 587).to_i,
  domain: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_DOMAIN", "localhost"),
  user_name: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_USERNAME", "apikey"),
  password: sendgrid_api_key,
  authentication: :plain,
  enable_starttls_auto: true,
  open_timeout: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_OPEN_TIMEOUT", 5).to_i,
  read_timeout: ENV.fetch("SENDGRID_READ_TIMEOUT", 5).to_i
}

ActionMailer::Base.default_url_options = {
  host: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST", "localhost"),
  protocol: ENV.fetch("APP_PROTOCOL", "http")
}

# config/environments/development.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
  config.action_mailer.perform_deliveries = true
  config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = true
  config.action_mailer.default_url_options = {
    host: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST", "localhost"),
    port: ENV.fetch("APP_PORT", 3000),
    protocol: ENV.fetch("APP_PROTOCOL", "http")
  }
end

# config/environments/production.rb
Rails.application.configure do
  config.action_mailer.delivery_method = :smtp
  config.action_mailer.perform_deliveries = true
  config.action_mailer.raise_delivery_errors = true
  config.action_mailer.default_url_options = {
    host: ENV.fetch("APP_HOST"),
    protocol: ENV.fetch("APP_PROTOCOL", "https")
  }
end

# Example usage:
# SendgridMailer.notify(
#   to: "user@example.com",
#   subject: "Welcome",
#   text_body: "Hello from Rails via SendGrid",
#   html_body: "<p>Hello from Rails via <strong>SendGrid</strong></p>",
#   categories: ["welcome-email"],
#   custom_args: { user_id: 123 }
# ).deliver_later
